title: Asterisk Security Log Brute-Force SIP Registration Attempts
app: Custom TA, HEC, or Universal Forwarder mapping Asterisk logs to `asterisk:pjsip`, `asterisk:cdr`, `asterisk:queue_log`, `asterisk:full`, `asterisk:messages`, `asterisk:security`, `asterisk:ami`
spl:
index=voip sourcetype="asterisk:security" earliest=-1h
| eval user=coalesce(user, username, account, "")
| eval src=coalesce(src_ip, remote_ip, SRC, "")
| where match(lower(_raw), "(?i)failed|invalid|reject|ban|wrong password|bad auth|not match")
| stats count dc(src) as sources values(src) as src_list by user
| where count>=15 OR sources>=5
description: High-velocity bad authentication against SIP registrations precedes toll fraud. Clustering by extension and source IP prioritizes blocks.
value: Reduces fraudulent usage charges and gives SOC an actionable VoIP-specific signal.
implementation: (1) Log client IP in security events. (2) Tune thresholds. (3) SOAR or firewall integration. (4) Allowlist scanners.
visualization: Table of users and IPs; sparkline volume.
grandmaExplanation: We spot attackers guessing phone passwords before they get in.
